question: Is the original white dial surface replaced with the gold/bronze radial guilloche pattern and blue Arabic numerals from Image 2?
Are the original silver watch hands from Image 1 preserved, remaining visible and intact?
Are the original 3D rectangular hour markers (indices) preserved with their realistic raised appearance?
Is the date window at the 3 o'clock position preserved and legible, without being covered by the new dial pattern?
Does the new dial pattern conform to the oblique perspective and slight convex curvature of the watch face in Image 1?
Is the edge of the new dial cleanly aligned with the inner bezel without gaps, jagged seams, or overlap?
Are the original bright white reflections and glares on the crystal from Image 1 preserved and visible *over* the new dial design?
Are the tree shadow details in the crystal reflection enhanced using the texture/pattern from Image 3?
Do the enhanced tree reflections appear natural and transparent, following the curvature of the crystal rather than looking like an opaque overlay?
Does the new dial pattern appear physically located underneath the watch hands, indices, and glass crystal (correct depth ordering)?
Do the preserved hands and indices cast appropriate contact shadows onto the new dial surface?
Is the sharpness, noise grain, and contrast of the new dial unified with the rest of the watch in Image 1?
Are the watch case, fluted bezel, bracelet, wrist, and background preserved without alteration or distortion?
Is the final image free from visible artifacts such as hallucinated geometry, floating elements, or harsh 'cut-and-paste' boundaries?
